bird.name: Ash-throated flycatcher
bird.summary: The ash-throated flycatcher (Myiarchus cinerascens) is a passerine bird in the tyrant flycatcher family. Taxonomy First described in 1851 b…

locations.name: 墨西哥
locations.summary: 墨西哥合众国（西班牙語：Estados Unidos Mexicanos），通稱墨西哥（西班牙語：México），是北美洲南部的一个主权国家，北接美国，西濱太平洋，南鄰伯利兹、危地马拉，东面墨西哥湾。其领土面积达近198万平方公里，为美洲…

relations.subject: Ash-throated flycatcher
relations.evidence: It breeds in desert scrub, riparian forest, brushy pastures and open woodland from the western United States to central Mexico

relations.subject: Ash-throated flycatcher
relations.evidence: Because of its extensive range, very large population, and generally increasing numbers, the ash-throated flycatcher has been listed as a species of least concern by the International Union for Conservation of Nature. It is one of the species protected by the Migratory Bird Treaty Act. In the United States, the creation of bluebird trails, a network of nest boxes put out for the continent's three bluebird species, may benefit ash-throated flycatchers as they will also use the boxes. Ash-throated flycatchers have been found to be negatively affected by noise pollution
relations.object_summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。
